author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
Caution Needed in Attending to Pelagius’ Deliverances on Infant Baptism.
title: Caution Needed in Attending to Pelagius’ Deliverances on Infant Baptism.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Chapter 1 [I.]—Caution Needed in Attending to Pelagius’ Deliverances on Infant Baptism.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Next I beg of you, carefully to observe with what caution you ought to lend an ear, on the question of the baptism of infants, to men of this character, who dare not openly deny the laver of regeneration and the forgiveness of sins to this early age, for fear that Christian ears would not bear to listen to them; and who yet persist in holding and urging their opinion, that the carnal generation is not held guilty of man’s first sin, although they seem to allow infants to be baptized for the remission of sins.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: You have, indeed, yourselves informed me in your letter, that you heard Pelagius say in your presence, reading out of that book of his which he declared that he had also sent to Rome, that they maintain that “infants ought to be baptized with the same formula of sacramental words as adults.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Who, after that statement, would suppose that one ought to raise any question at all on this subject?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Or if he did, to whom would he not seem to indulge a very calumnious disposition—previous to the perusal of their plain assertions, in which they deny that infants inherit original sin, and contend that all persons are born free from all corruption?
